Description: Components re-render when props or state change even if the output would be identical
Do: Use React.memo for functional components that receive the same props frequently
Don't: Wrap every component in React.memo without profiling first
Code Example Good:
const MemoizedCard = React.memo(function Card({ title, data }) { return
{title}
; });

Description: Creating new function instances on every render causes child components to re-render
Do: Define handlers outside render or use useCallback for stable references

Description: Object literals create new references on every render triggering unnecessary updates
Do: Use useMemo for object props or define objects outside component

Description: Multiple setState calls in event handlers can cause multiple re-renders in older React
Do: Use functional updates and batch related state changes together

Description: Storing derived values in state creates sync issues and extra renders
Do: Calculate derived values during render using useMemo
Don't: const total = useMemo(() => items.reduce((a b) => a + b.price 0) [items]);

Description: Putting all state in global stores causes unnecessary component updates
Do: Keep state as close to where it is used as possible
Don't: Use useState for component-local state and context for shared state
Code Example Good: Put every piece of state in Redux/Zustand regardless of scope

Description: Sequential data fetching in useEffect creates loading waterfalls
Do: Use parallel fetching with Promise.all or React Query's useQueries

Description: Multiple components fetching the same data waste bandwidth and cause inconsistencies
Do: Use SWR or React Query for automatic request deduplication and caching

Description: Accessing state in effects without proper dependencies leads to stale values
Do: Include all dependencies in the dependency array or use functional updates

Description: Importing entire libraries when only using a few functions bloats bundle size
Do: Use tree-shakeable imports or lighter alternatives

Description: Loading all JavaScript upfront delays initial page load
Do: Use dynamic imports for routes and heavy components
Don't: const Chart = dynamic(() => import('./Chart'), { ssr: false });

Description: Dependencies that are no longer used still contribute to bundle size
Do: Regularly audit and remove unused dependencies
Don't: Run npm-check or depcheck to find unused packages

Description: Large unoptimized images slow down page load and waste bandwidth
Do: Use next/image for automatic optimization and lazy loading

Description: Images without dimensions cause layout shifts as they load
Do: Always specify width and height or use aspect-ratio CSS

Description: Hero images loading late hurt Largest Contentful Paint scores
Do: Add priority prop to above-the-fold images

Description: Missing or unstable keys cause React to recreate DOM elements unnecessarily
Do: Use stable unique identifiers as keys

Description: Using array index as key causes bugs when list items are reordered or filtered
Do: Use unique stable IDs from your data as keys

Description: Rendering thousands of list items at once causes jank and memory issues
Do: Use virtualization libraries for lists over 100 items

Description: Not cleaning up subscriptions and timers causes memory leaks
Do: Return cleanup function from useEffect
Don't: useEffect(() => { const id = setInterval(fn, 1000); return () => clearInterval(id); }, []);

Description: Async operations in useEffect can complete after unmount causing errors
Do: Use AbortController or data fetching libraries with cancellation
Don't: useEffect(() => { const controller = new AbortController(); fetch(url, { signal: controller.signal }); return () => controller.abort(); }, [url]);

Description: Fetching data too frequently wastes resources and can hit rate limits
Do: Use appropriate intervals and implement request throttling
Don't: Set polling interval based on data freshness requirements
Code Example Good: Poll every second for data that changes hourly

Description: Placing providers too high in the tree causes unnecessary re-renders
Do: Place context providers as close to consumers as possible
Don't: Wrap only the components that need the context
Code Example Good: Wrap entire app in every context provider at root level

Description: Putting many values in one context causes all consumers to re-render on any change
Do: Split context by update frequency or use context selectors
Don't: Create separate contexts for theme and user and settings
Code Example Good: Put theme user settings and everything in one AppContext

Description: Context value objects recreated on every render cause consumer re-renders
Do: Memoize context values with useMemo

Description: Switching between controlled and uncontrolled inputs causes warnings and bugs
Do: Choose controlled or uncontrolled and be consistent
Don't: Always provide value and onChange together for controlled inputs
Code Example Good: Start with defaultValue then add value prop later

Description: Updating parent state on every keystroke causes excessive re-renders
Do: Use uncontrolled inputs with refs or form libraries like React Hook Form
Code Example Good: const [name, setName] = useState(''); <input value={name} onChange={e => setName(e.target.value)} />

Description: Client-side validation improves UX and reduces unnecessary server requests
Do: Implement validation with clear error messages and aria attributes
Don't: Use schema validation with Zod or Yup and display errors inline
Code Example Good: Only validate on server after form submission

Description: Using use client unnecessarily prevents server-side rendering benefits
Do: Only add use client when component needs browser APIs or state
Don't: Keep data fetching and static content in Server Components
Code Example Good: Add use client to every component by default

Description: Passing non-serializable props across server/client boundary causes errors
Do: Only pass serializable data (strings numbers objects arrays) to Client Components
Don't: Pass primitive props and fetch data in Server Components
Code Example Good: Pass functions or class instances to Client Components

Description: Not using Suspense with async Server Components shows nothing during load
Do: Wrap async components in Suspense with meaningful fallbacks

Description: Server and client rendering different content causes hydration errors
Do: Ensure consistent rendering between server and client
Don't: Use useEffect for client-only code or suppressHydrationWarning
Code Example Good: Use Date.now() or Math.random() in initial render

Description: Accessing browser APIs during SSR causes errors
Do: Check for window existence or use useEffect for browser APIs

Description: Loading third-party scripts incorrectly blocks hydration
Do: Use next/script with appropriate loading strategy

Description: Using anchor tags instead of Link causes full page reloads
Do: Use next/link for internal navigation to enable client-side routing

Description: Not prefetching likely navigation targets slows perceived performance
Do: Use Link with prefetch for important navigation paths
Code Example Good: Disable prefetch on all links without consideration

Description: Not caching route handlers causes unnecessary data fetching
Do: Use appropriate cache and revalidation settings
Code Example Good: Fetch fresh data on every request without caching

Description: Animating layout properties like width and height causes expensive reflows
Do: Animate transform and opacity for smooth 60fps animations

Description: Browsers can optimize animations when given hints about what will change
Do: Add will-change for complex animations but remove after
Code Example Good: Never use will-change or use it on too many elements

Description: Some users are sensitive to motion and animations
Do: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query
Code Example Good: Use animations everywhere without motion preference check

Description: Adding event listeners without removing them causes memory leaks
Do: Remove event listeners in useEffect cleanup
Don't: useEffect(() => { window.addEventListener('resize', fn); return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', fn); }, []);

Description: Storing large nested objects in state makes updates expensive
Do: Normalize state and use IDs to reference related data
Don't: Store entities by ID in flat objects with arrays of IDs
Code Example Good: Deeply nest all related data in single state object

Description: Closures capturing large objects prevent garbage collection
Do: Be mindful of what closures capture and clean up references
Don't: Use refs for values that should not be captured in closures
Code Example Good: Capture large arrays or objects in long-lived callbacks

Description: Using any type defeats TypeScript benefits and hides bugs
Do: Define proper types for props state and function parameters
Don't: interface Props { items: Item[]; onSelect: (item: Item) => void; }

Description: Types that are too broad do not catch errors at compile time
Do: Use specific union types and generics for type safety
